references.notes: These verses describe the act of burial and covering the deceased with earth, which are specific mortuary practices.
The term 'dahr' (time/fate) is used here in a sense that aligns with pre-Islamic cosmological concepts of time as an influential force.
The personification of 'manīya' (fate/death) as an assailing force reflects pre-Islamic cosmological beliefs about fate.
This verse personifies death as an active agent, which is consistent with pre-Islamic cosmological understandings of death as a powerful force.
The term 'al-qadr' (fate/destiny) is used here to denote an uncontrollable, pre-ordained aspect of life, aligning with pre-Islamic cosmological concepts.
This verse describes the use of rock slabs in constructing the grave, a detail of pre-Islamic burial practices.
